subject: Mathematics
solution:
We have to find l=limθ→0tanπcos2θsin2πsin2θ

Using sin2θ+cos2θ=1, we get

⇒l=limθ→0tanπ1-sin2θsin2πsin2θ

⇒l=limθ→0tanπ-πsin2θsin2πsin2θ

Using tanπ-θ=-tanθ, we get

⇒l=limθ→0-tanπsin2θsin2πsin2θ

⇒l=limθ→0-tanπsin2θπsin2θ2πsin2θsin2πsin2θ×12

Using, limx→0sinxx=1 & limx→0tanxx=1, we get

l=-12.

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given that

⇒limx→0eax-cos(bx)-cx2e-cx1-cos2x=17

We know that eax=1+ax+(ax)22!+… and cosbx=1-(bx)22!+…

⇒limx→01+ax+ax22!+…-1-bx22!+…-cx21-cx+cx22!-…1-cos2x2x2×4x2=17

⇒limx→0a-c2x+a2+b2+c22x2+…12×4x2=17

Now for limit to exist, a-c2=0⇒c=2a

⇒limx→0a2+b2+c22x2+…12×4x2=17

⇒a2+b2+c24=17

⇒a2+b2+4a2=68

⇒5a2+b2=68

Hence this is the correct option.

solution:

subject: Mathematics
solution:
$\begin{aligned} \frac{\alpha}{5 e} & =\exp \left(\lim _{t \rightarrow 0} \frac{1}{t}\left(\int_0^1(3 x+5)^t d x-1\right)\right) \\ & =\exp \left(\lim _{t \rightarrow 0} \frac{1}{t}\left(\frac{(3 x+5)^{t+1}}{3(t+1)}\right)_0^1-1\right) \\ & =\exp \left(\lim _{t \rightarrow 0} \frac{1}{t}\left(\frac{8^{t+1}-5^{t+1}}{3(t+1)}-1\right)\right) \\ & =\exp \left(\lim _{t \rightarrow 0} \frac{1}{t}\left(\frac{8^{t+1}-5^{t+1}-3 t-3}{3(t+1)}\right)\right) \\ & =\exp \left(\lim _{t \rightarrow 0}\left(\frac{8^{t+1} \cdot \ln 8-5^{t+1} \ln 5-3}{3(t+1)}\right)\right) \\ & =\exp \left(\frac{\ln 8^8-\ln 5^5-3}{5}\right)\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & =\left(\frac{8}{5}\right)^{2 / 3} \frac{\alpha}{5 e}=\exp \left(\frac{\ln \left(\frac{8^8}{5^5}\right)}{5}-1\right) \\ & \Rightarrow\left(\frac{8}{5}\right)^{2 / 3} \frac{\alpha}{5}=\left(\frac{8^8}{5^5}\right)^{1 / 3}=\left(\frac{8^6 \cdot 8^2}{5^3 \cdot 5^2}\right)^{1 / 3}=\frac{64}{5}\left(\frac{8}{5}\right)^{2 / 3} \\ & \Rightarrow \alpha=64\end{aligned}$

options: <math><munder><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>→</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></munder><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> exists but <math><munder><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>→</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></munder><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> does not exist

<math><munder><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>→</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></munder><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> exists but <math><munder><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>→</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></munder><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> does not exist

Both <math><munder><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>→</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></munder><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math><munder><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>→</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></munder><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> exist but are not equal
solution:

L.H.L.=limx→4-x-x4=3-0=3

fx=4-1=3

L.H.L.=f4=R.H.L.

Hence, given function is continuous at x=4.

solution:

⇒limx→0x+x33!…-x-x33…3x3

⇒limx→01+3x23!…-1-3x23…9x2 (Use L'Hospital rule 00 form)

⇒limx→06x3!+…--6x3+…18x  (Use L'Hospital rule 00 form)

⇒limx→063!+…--63+…18=1+218=16

Given that, limx→0sin-1x-tan-1x3x3=L

∴ 16=L⇒6L=1

So, 6L+1=2

subject: Mathematics
solution:
RHL is, $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0^{+}} \frac{\tan \left(\pi \sin ^{2} x\right)+(x-0)^{2}}{x^{2}}$

$=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0^{+}}\left(\frac{\tan \left(\pi \sin ^{2} x\right.}{x^{2}}+1\right)=1+\pi$

And LHL is, $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0^{-}} \frac{\tan \left(\pi \sin ^{2} x\right)+(-x+\sin x)^{2}}{x^{2}}$

$=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0^{-}} \frac{\tan \left(\pi \sin ^{2} x\right)+x^{2}+\sin ^{2} x-2 x \sin x}{x^{2}}$

$=\pi+1+1-2=\pi$

Since, LHL $\neq$ RHL Hence, limit does not exist.

subject: Mathematics
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \lim _{x \rightarrow \frac{1}{a}} 16 \cdot \frac{\left(1-\cos 2\left(x-\frac{1}{a}\right)\left(x-\frac{1}{b}\right)\right)}{4\left(x-\frac{1}{b}\right)^2} \times \frac{4\left(x-\frac{1}{b}\right)^2}{a^2\left(x-\frac{1}{a}\right)^2} \\ & =16 \times \frac{2}{a^2}\left(\frac{1}{a}-\frac{1}{b}\right)^2 \\ & =\frac{32}{a^2}\left(\frac{17}{4}\right)=\frac{17.8}{a^2}=\frac{17 \times 8 \times 16}{(-1+\sqrt{117})^2} \\ & =\frac{136.16}{18.2 \sqrt{7}} \times \frac{18+2 \sqrt{7}}{18+2 \sqrt{7}} \\ & =\frac{136}{256}(18+2 \sqrt{7}) \cdot 16 \\ & =153+17 \sqrt{17}=\alpha+\beta \sqrt{17} \\ & \alpha+\beta=153+17=170\end{aligned}$

solution:
Let,

L=limn→∞212-213212-215....212-212n+1

Now,

 212-213<1

212-215<1

--------------------

212-212n+1<1   ∀n∈N

And 212-213n<212-213212-215....212-212n+1<212-212n+1n

⇒limn→∞212-213n<limn→∞212-213212-215....212-212n+1<limn→∞212-212n+1n

⇒limn→∞212-213n<L<limn→∞212-212n+1n

And limn→∞212-213n=0  & limn→∞212-212n+1n=0

as  212-213<1 & 212-212n+1<1 

Hence,  limn→∞212-213212-215....212-212n+1=0

solution:
We have,

limx→0αxex-βloge(1+x)+γx2e-xxsin2x=10

⇒limx→0αx1+x+x22!+..-βx-x22+x33-...+γx21-x+x22!-..x3sin2xx2=10

⇒limx→0αx1+x+x22-βx-x22+x33+γx21-xx3=10

Neglecting higher powers.

limx→0x(α-β)+x2α+β2+γ+x3α2-β3-γx3=10

For limit to exist

α-β=0

α+β2+γ=0

α2-β3-γ=10 …i

β=α,γ=-3α2

Putting in i, we get

α2-α3+3α2=10

⇒α6+3α2=10

⇒α+9α6=10

⇒α=6

Hence,

α=6,β=6,γ=-9

⇒α+β+γ=3

$\lim _{x \rightarrow \frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{x}{2}\right) \cdot(1-\sin x)}{(\pi-2 x)^3}$
Let $\mathrm{x}=\frac{\pi}{2}+\mathrm{y} ; \mathrm{y} \rightarrow 0$
$=\lim _{y \rightarrow 0} \frac{-\tan \left(-\frac{y}{2}\right) \cdot(1-\cos y)}{(-2 y)^3}=\lim _{y \rightarrow 0} \frac{-\tan \frac{y}{2} 2 \sin ^2 \frac{y}{2}}{(-8) \cdot \frac{y^3}{8} \cdot 8}$
$=\lim _{y \rightarrow 0} \frac{1}{32} \frac{\tan \frac{y}{2}}{\left(\frac{y}{2}\right)} \cdot\left[\frac{\sin y / 2}{y / 2}\right]^2=\frac{1}{32}$

subject: Mathematics
solution:
$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty}\left[\frac{1}{n^2} \sec ^2 \frac{1}{n^2}+\frac{2}{n^2} \sec ^2 \frac{4}{n^2}+\frac{3}{n^2} \sec ^2 \frac{9}{n^2}+\ldots .+\frac{1}{n} \sec ^2 1\right]$ is equal to $\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{r}{n^2} \sec ^2 \frac{r^2}{n^2}=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{n} \cdot \frac{r}{n} \sec ^2 \frac{r^2}{n^2}$
$\Rightarrow$ Given limit is equal to value of integral $\int_0^1 x \sec ^2 x^2 d x$
or $\frac{1}{2} \int_0^1 2 x \sec x^2 d x=\frac{1}{2} \int_0^1 \sec ^2 t d t$
[put $\left.x^2=t\right]$ $=\frac{1}{2}(\tan t)_0^1=\frac{1}{2} \tan 1$.

subject: Mathematics
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \lim _{x \rightarrow \frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{0-\{\sin (2 x)+\cos (x)\} \cdot 3 x^2}{2\left(x-\frac{\pi}{2}\right)} \\ & =\lim _{x \rightarrow \frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{-\{2 \sin x \cos x+\cos x\} 3 x^2}{2\left(x-\frac{\pi}{2}\right)} \\ & =\lim _{x \rightarrow \frac{\pi}{2}}\left\{\frac{2 \sin x \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)}{2\left(x-\frac{\pi}{2}\right)}+\frac{\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)}{2\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)}\right\} 3 x^2 \\ & =\left(1(1)+\frac{1}{2}\right) 3\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)^2 \\ & =\frac{9 \pi^2}{8}\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Let $T = \lim_{x \to 1} \left( \dfrac{f(x+2)}{f(3)} \right)^{\dfrac{18}{(x-1)^2}}$ ; $1^{\infty}$ form
$\Rightarrow T = e^{\lim_{x \to 1} \dfrac{18}{(x-1)^2} \left( \dfrac{f(x+2) - f(3)}{f(3)} \right)}$
$\Rightarrow T = e^{\lim_{x \to 1} \dfrac{18}{(x-1)^2} \left( \dfrac{f(x+2) - f(3)}{18} \right)}$
$\Rightarrow T = e^{\lim_{x \to 1} \left( \dfrac{f(x+2) - f(3)}{(x-1)^2} \right)}$ $\dfrac{0}{0}$ form, apply L'pital
$\Rightarrow T = e^{\lim_{x \to 1} \dfrac{f'(x+2)}{2(x-1)}}$ ; $\dfrac{0}{0}$ form, apply L'pital
$\Rightarrow T = e^{\lim_{x \to 1} \dfrac{f''(x+2)}{2}}$ $= e^{\dfrac{4}{2}} = e^2$
$\Rightarrow \log_e(T) = 2$

=elimx→01-cosxcos2xx2×x+2
Now,

Using L'Hospital Rule

limx→0sinxcos2x-cosx×12cos2x×-2sin2x2x
 

=limx→0sinxcos2x+sin2x·cosx2xcos2x

=limx→0sin3x2xcos2x

=32limx→0sin3x3x×limx→01cos2x

=32limx→0sin3x3x×1

=32

So,

elimx→01-cosxcos2xx2x+2

=e32×2=e3

⇒a=3

solution:
$\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)\left[\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x+1}{2 x+1}\right)-\frac{\pi}{4}\right]$
$=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)\left[\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x+1}{2 x+1}\right)-\tan ^{-1}(1)\right]$
$=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right) \cdot \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\frac{x+1}{2 x+1}-1}{1+\frac{x+1}{2 x+1}}\right)$
$=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{1}{x} \cdot \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{-x}{3 x+2}\right)$
$=-\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left[\frac{\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{3 x+2}\right)}{\frac{x}{3 x+2}} \times \frac{1}{3 x+2}\right]=-\frac{1}{2}$

subject: Mathematics
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \alpha=\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty}\left(\left(\frac{\mathrm{e}}{1-\mathrm{e}}\right)\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{e}}-\frac{\mathrm{x}}{1+\mathrm{x}}\right)\right)^{\mathrm{x}}\left(1^{\infty} \text { form }\right) \\ & \therefore \alpha=\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{L}} \\ & \text { Where } \mathrm{L}=\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} \mathrm{x}\left(\left(\frac{\mathrm{e}}{1-\mathrm{e}}\right)\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{e}}-\frac{\mathrm{x}}{1+\mathrm{x}}\right)-1\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{L}=\lim _{\mathrm{x} \rightarrow \infty}\left(\frac{\mathrm{e}}{1-\mathrm{e}}\right) \mathrm{x}\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{e}}-\frac{\mathrm{x}}{1+\mathrm{x}}-\left(\frac{1-\mathrm{e}}{\mathrm{e}}\right)\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{L}=\frac{\mathrm{e}}{1-\mathrm{e}} \lim _{\mathrm{x} \rightarrow \infty} \mathrm{x}\left(1-\frac{\mathrm{x}}{1+\mathrm{x}}\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{L}=\frac{\mathrm{e}}{1-\mathrm{e}} \lim _{\mathrm{x} \rightarrow \infty} \frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{x}+1} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{~L}=\frac{\mathrm{e}}{1-\mathrm{e}} \cdot 1 \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{~L}=\frac{\mathrm{e}}{1-\mathrm{e}} \\ & \therefore \alpha=\mathrm{e}^{\frac{\mathrm{e}}{1-\mathrm{e}}} \Rightarrow \log \alpha=\frac{\mathrm{e}}{1-\mathrm{e}} \\ & \therefore \text { Required value }=\frac{\frac{\mathrm{e}}{1-\mathrm{e}}}{1+\frac{\mathrm{e}}{1-\mathrm{e}}}=\mathrm{e}\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{\sum_{r=1}^{n-1}\left(r^2-r\right)(n-r)}{\sum_{r=1}^n r^3-\sum_{r=1}^n r^2} \\ & \lim _{\mathrm{n} \rightarrow \infty} \frac{\sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{\mathrm{n}-1}\left(-\mathrm{r}^3+\mathrm{r}^2(\mathrm{n}+1)-\mathrm{nr}\right)}{\left(\frac{\mathrm{n}(\mathrm{n}+1)}{2}\right)^2-\frac{\mathrm{n}(\mathrm{n}+1)(2 \mathrm{n}+1)}{6}} \\ & \lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{\left(\frac{((n-1) n)}{2}\right)^2+\frac{(n+1)(n-1) n(2 n-1)}{6}-\frac{n^2(n-1)}{2}}{\frac{n(n+1)}{2}\left(\frac{n(n+1)}{2}-\frac{2 n+1}{3}\right)} \\ & \lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{\frac{n(n-1)}{2}\left(\frac{-n(n-1)}{2}+\frac{(n+1)(2 n-1)}{3}-n\right)}{\frac{n(n+1)}{2} \frac{3 n^2+3 n-4 n-2}{6}} \\ & \lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{(n-1)\left(-3 n^2+3 n+2\left(2 n^2+n-1\right)-6\right)}{(n+1)\left(3 n^2-n-2\right)} \\ & \lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{(n-1)\left(n^2+5 n-8\right)}{(n+1)\left(3 n^2-n-2\right)}=\frac{1}{3} \\ & \end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Put } x=1+h \\ & \lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{h(6+\lambda \cosh )-\mu \sinh }{h^3}=-1 \\ & \lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{h\left(6+\lambda\left(1-\frac{h^2}{2!}\right)\right)-\mu\left(h-\frac{h^3}{3!}\right)}{h^3}=-1 \\ & 6+\lambda-\mu=0 \text { and }-\frac{\lambda}{2}+\frac{\mu}{6}=-1 \\ & \lambda+\mu=18\end{aligned}$
